name: T.S. Eliot

acts.description: Anti-Semitic themes and imagery present in major early poems; stated in a 1934 lecture that a community should not have too many Jews
Institutional cruelty to first wife Vivienne Haigh-Wood, committed to asylum and refused to visit

behavior_summary: One of the greatest poets in English had documented anti-Semitic views and abandoned his mentally ill first wife to die in an asylum without visiting her for years.

notable_works: The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock
art.summary: The Waste Land is possibly the most important poem in English of the 20th century. Nobel Prize winner. Transformed what poetry could do.

verdict.blurb: He wrote The Waste Land and gave us Prufrock and had Gerontion say things about Jews that made Christopher Ricks write an entire scholarly book parsing the anti-Semitism. He had his first wife committed and didn't visit. The poetry is transcendent. The man was cold-blooded.
